prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Use the latest DGA record for transformer T-012 to identify the most likely fault mode and explain the top two candidate failure mechanisms.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should map DGA evidence to a primary fault mode and provide at least one alternative candidate with rationale grounded in gas patterns or known fault mapping.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Retrieve the latest DGA record for transformer T-012
  Step 2: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Analyze the DGA record to identify the most likely fault mode (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search for failure modes related to the identified fault mode (depends on steps [2])
  Step 4: [fmsr] none — Get the top two candidate failure mechanisms from the search results (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [fmsr] get_sensor_correlation — Explain the top two candidate failure mechanisms (depends on steps [4])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the latest DGA record for transformer T-012",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_dga_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-012"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-012\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-09T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 41.98,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 48.59,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 3.36,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 19.78,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 9.53,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 369.42,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 2201.98,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Low-temperature overheating\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Analyze the DGA record to identify the most likely fault mode",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "analyze_dga",
    "tool_args": {
      "h2": 41.98,
      "ch4": 48.59,
      "c2h2": 3.36,
      "c2h4": 19.78,
      "c2h6": 9.53,
      "transformer_id": "T-012"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"iec_code\": \"N\",\n  \"diagnosis\": \"Normal / Inconclusive\",\n  \"r1_ch4_h2\": 1.1575,\n  \"r2_c2h2_c2h4\": 0.1699,\n  \"r3_c2h4_c2h6\": 2.0756,\n  \"input_gases\": {\n    \"h2_ppm\": 41.98,\n    \"ch4_ppm\": 48.59,\n    \"c2h2_ppm\": 3.36,\n    \"c2h4_ppm\": 19.78,\n    \"c2h6_ppm\": 9.53\n  },\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-012\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true
  },
  {
    "step": 3,
    "task": "Search for failure modes related to the identified fault mode",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "search_failure_modes",
    "tool_args": {
      "query": "Low-temperature overheating"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"failure_mode_id\": \"FM-002\",\n  \"name\": \"Low-Temperature Overheating (< 300\\u00b0C)\",\n  \"severity\": \"medium\",\n  \"iec_code\": \"T1\",\n  \"key_gases\": \"CH4,C2H4\",\n  \"recommended_action\": \"Reduce load; inspect within 30 days\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true
  },
  {
    "step": 4,
    "task": "Get the top two candidate failure mechanisms from the search results",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "none",
    "tool_args": {},
    "response": "Top two candidate failure mechanisms",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true
  },
  {
    "step": 5,
    "task": "Explain the top two candidate failure mechanisms",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_sensor_correlation",
    "tool_args": {
      "failure_mode_id": "FM-002"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"failure_mode_id\": \"FM-002\",\n  \"name\": \"Low-Temperature Overheating (< 300\\u00b0C)\",\n  \"dga_label\": \"Low-temperature overheating\",\n  \"description\": \"Low-temperature thermal fault, typically in core laminations or due to overload. Elevated CH4 and C2H4.\",\n  \"severity\": \"medium\",\n  \"iec_code\": \"T1\",\n  \"key_gases\": [\n    \"CH4\",\n    \"C2H4\"\n  ],\n  \"recommended_action\": \"Reduce load; inspect within 30 days\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
The most likely fault mode for transformer T-012 is Low-Temperature Overheating (< 300°C). The top two candidate failure mechanisms are not explicitly listed as two separate mechanisms, but the primary failure mechanism identified is Low-Temperature Overheating, characterized by elevated levels of CH4 and C2H4, indicating a low-temperature thermal fault, typically in core laminations or due to overload, with a recommended action to reduce load and inspect within 30 days.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": true,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true,
    "generalized_result_verification": false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": false,
    "clarity_and_justification": false,
    "hallucinations": true,
    "suggestions": "Improve the agent's ability to provide explicit and separate failure mechanisms and enhance the sequence logic to handle such tasks effectively."
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Improve the agent's ability to provide explicit and separate failure mechanisms and enhance the sequence logic to handle such tasks effectively.
